description: Archived discoveries capture the validated learning that shaped current long-haul airline delivery priorities.

riskFramework.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
Can customers figure out how to use it?
Can we build it within given resources and constraints?

items.title: Premium disruption recovery: validated discovery
items.name: Premium disruption recovery: validated discovery
items.summary: Discovery confirmed that premium guest trust is damaged less by the disruption event itself than by fragmented, low-clarity recovery across app, airport, and service channels.
items.opportunity: Clarify whether linking operations-control signals more directly into guest self-service and assisted servicing would materially improve recovery confidence and reduce handling cost.

linkedInitiatives.description: Premium disruption recovery initiative focused on improving disruption recovery & self-service reaccommodation, trip companion & proactive notifications, operational control tower, and customer care & case management across the Guest Mobile App, Proactive Messaging Layer, Network Control Workspace, and Disruption Servicing Workspace.

assignments.how: Led disruption replay analysis and defined the operating decision points that need product support.
Validated guest communication and self-service failure modes in disrupted journeys.
Validated where airport and contact-centre handoffs break down in real recovery cases.

riskFocus.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Premium guests will trust the airline more if disruption recovery becomes visibly proactive, digital-first, and context aware.
evidenceToCollect: Whether guests describe status clarity and actionable recovery options as the main unmet need during disruption.
Whether better digital recovery materially changes willingness to rebook or recommend.

riskFocus.question: Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Lower call demand and less compensation fallout will justify investment in a joined-up recovery model.
evidenceToCollect: How much handling cost is driven by avoidable guest confusion.
Which disruption cohorts create the highest cost and retention damage.

riskFocus.question: Can customers figure out how to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Guests can successfully self-serve more disruption scenarios if the recovery options are ranked and explained clearly.
evidenceToCollect: Where guests hesitate in prototype rebooking flows.

riskFocus.question: Can we build it within given resources and constraints?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The teams can join operational risk and guest recovery context without replacing every legacy operations tool at once.
evidenceToCollect: Which operational signals are essential to expose first.
How much recovery logic can be externalized from legacy systems.

plannedActivities: Replay recent long-haul cancellation and misconnections cases across app, airport, and care teams.
Prototype a guest-facing recovery journey driven by network-control signals.
Define the smallest joined-up disruption slice for current-quarter delivery.
items.decisionToMake: Decide whether to fund a guest-aware disruption recovery initiative rather than treating operations and servicing separately.

outcome.summary: Discovery found enough guest pain, operational leverage, and cost reduction opportunity to proceed.

items.title: Holiday unification: validated discovery
items.name: Holiday unification: validated discovery
items.summary: Discovery confirmed that holiday buyers experience the product as fragmented when package shopping, payments, traveller details, and pre-travel readiness are handled in separate service models.
items.opportunity: Clarify whether a more unified holiday retail and servicing model would improve package conversion, lower pre-travel support demand, and strengthen repeat leisure demand.

linkedInitiatives.description: Holidays retail and servicing unification initiative focused on improving holidays packaging & fulfilment, payments, refunds & revenue settlement, identity, profile & document readiness, and trip companion & proactive notifications across Holidays Search and Booking Experience and Holiday Servicing Workspace.

assignments.how: Led leisure-journey analysis and package-servicing problem framing.
Validated payment, refund, and traveller-detail friction across package bookings.

riskFocus.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Families will convert more often if holiday value, payment timing, and travel readiness are explained as one joined-up offer.
evidenceToCollect: Whether unclear payment and readiness steps drive holiday abandonment.
Which aspects of package clarity most affect family confidence.

riskFocus.question: Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Higher conversion and lower servicing cost create enough margin upside to justify investment in a stronger package model.
evidenceToCollect: What portion of package service demand is caused by avoidable ambiguity.
How conversion and margin shift when package flows are simplified.

riskFocus.question: Can customers figure out how to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Holiday buyers can self-serve more of the booking and preparation journey if the system clearly shows what is included, what is due, and what remains to be completed.
evidenceToCollect: Where users fail in traveller-detail and balance-payment steps.
Which package states are most confusing in current flows.

riskFocus.question: Can we build it within given resources and constraints?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The teams can unify the highest-value package flows without replacing every supplier integration and legacy service process at once.
evidenceToCollect: Which supplier and payment dependencies block unification.
Which holiday workflows can be standardized first.

plannedActivities: Review abandoned package quotes and pre-travel support cases.
Prototype a clearer package-payment-readiness journey for family bookings.
Define the first unified slice covering booking, staged payment, and travel-readiness prompts.
items.decisionToMake: Decide whether flights and holidays should share a more unified servicing and readiness model.

outcome.summary: Discovery confirmed enough customer and margin upside to move ahead with unification work.
